ocr.text: In the fiscal year 2023, 2U Inc. presented a comprehensive overview of online course completion rates across various programs,
providing valuable insights into enrollment, performance metrics, and overall trends in the digital education landscape. The
table began with the course name "DataScience," which had a total enrollment of 1,500 students, achieving a completion rate of
85%, a dropout rate of 15%, and an average score of 78. Following this, the "BusinessManagement" course recorded a total
enrollment of 2,000 students, with a completion rate of 82%, a dropout rate of 18%, and an average score of 75. The
"Cybersecurity" course attracted a total of 1,200 enrolled students, achieving a notable completion rate of 90%, a low dropout
rate of 10%, and an average score of 80. The "DigitalMarketing" program saw 1,800 students enrolled, with an impressive
completion rate of 88%, a dropout rate of 12%, and an average score of 82.
CourseName  TotalEnrolled  CompletionRates  DropoutRates  AverageScore
DataScience  1500  85%  15%  78  BusinessManagement  2000
82%  18%  75  Cybersecurity  1200  90%  10%
80  DigitalMarketing  1800  88%  12%  82  AIFundamentals
1000  92%  8%  85  TotalCourses  7000  86%
14%  78  AverageEnrollment  1400  85%  15%  Overall
"AIFundamentals" had a total enrollment of 1,000 students, yielding a completion rate of 92%, an 8% dropout rate, and an
average score of 85. Aggregating these data points, the total across all courses amounted to 7,000 enrolled students, culminating

in an overall completion rate of 86%, a dropout rate of 14%, and an average score of 78. 

This detailed analysis not only reflects
individual course performance but also highlights trends in enrollment and completion rates that are critical for understanding
learner engagement and program effectiveness. The "DataScience" course's completion rate of 85% and dropout rate of 15%
suggest a balanced student retention, while "BusinessManagement" shows opportunities for improvement with its 82%
completion rate and 18% dropout rate. Notably, "Cybersecurity" stands out with a high completion rate of 90% and a low
dropout rate of 10%, indicating strong student satisfaction and engagement. The "DigitalMarketing" course's metrics reflect a
robust completion rate of 88%, complemented by a manageable dropout rate of 12%, suggesting that the program is effectively
meeting learner needs. In contrast, "AIFundamentals" demonstrates high student success with a completion rate of 92% and a

minimal dropout rate of 8%, representing an exemplary model for course design and delivery. 

The total courses section, which
encapsulates data from all five programs, confirms that 7,000 students were enrolled across the board, with an overall
completion rate of 86% and a dropout rate of 14%. This collective performance indicates a strong capacity for course delivery
within the organization, while the average score of 78 further emphasizes areas where additional focus may be warranted for
future curriculum development. The average enrollment across the courses was computed at 1,400, reinforcing the diversity of
student engagement across different fields of study. The data reveals a commitment to educational excellence, with completion

rates consistently above 80% for all courses, signalling effective instructional strategies and support systems in place. 

Overall,
the FY2023 completion rates present a promising landscape for 2U Inc., reflecting both the strengths and areas for potential
enhancement within their online learning offerings, as well as providing critical data for strategic planning and resource
allocation moving forward in the education technology sector.
